mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show zero statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicates demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, creating negative forward equity returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually while RSAFS growth remains below 2%, or interaction term IC is negative over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in growth.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue bases and improves profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the signal loses significance when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that supports consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when payroll growth exceeds 300k but unemployment remains flat, or interaction IC turns negative during demographic shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units signals a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, transmitting negative sentiment to broader equity markets over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines below 1000.0 fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal becomes positive when housing supply constraints dominate demand weakness.
